feed.title: Microsoft Updates

entry.title: Version 2508: August 26
entry.content: Version 2508 (Build 19127.20154) Feature updates Office Suite New Trust Center settings to block file opens using insecure protocols: New settings in Trust Center allow users to restrict or allow file opens using FTP/HTTP protocols and fallback to FrontPage Remote Procedure Call (FPRPC) protocol. Resolved issues Outlook Fixed an issue that caused Outlook to terminate unexpectedly when sending a meeting invite with an encryption label. Office Suite We added support for pixelated rendering of embe

entry.content: Version 2508 (Build 19127.20192) Resolved issues Outlook Fixed an issue where some Outlook add-ins were encountering an "Office.auth.getAccessToken is not a function" error. Fixed an issue where users received unexpected meeting cancellations following meeting exception updates.

entry.title: Version 2602: February 24
entry.content: Version 2602 (Build 19725.20126) Resolved issues OneNote Fixed an issue that caused OneNote to close unexpectedly upon startup. Fixed an issue that caused OneNote to close unexpectedly when copying and pasting content from a web page containing a (MathML) tag with no attributes. Outlook Updated how Word, Excel, and PowerPoint retrieve configuration information to allow People experiences to work properly in Sovereign cloud environments.

entry.title: Outlook (new) release notes: June 12, 2026
entry.content: PST import now supports all item types, including calendar events, contacts, and tasks.

entry.title: Windows Office Hours: June 18, 2026
entry.content: If you are an IT admin with questions about managing and updating Windows, we want to help. Every third Thursday of the month, we host a live chat-based event on the Tech Community called Windows Office Hours. Members of the Windows, Microsoft Intune, Windows Autopilot, Windows Autopatch, and Windows 365 engineering teams will be standing by to answer your questions.

entry.title: The June 2026 Windows security update is available
entry.content: The June 2026 security update was released for all supported versions of Windows on Tuesday, June 9, at 10 am PT, for all supported versions of Windows. We recommend that you install these updates promptly. Information about the contents of this update is available from the release notes, which are accessible from the Windows 11 update history page. For instructions on how to install this update on your home device, check the Update Windows article. To learn more about the different types of mon

entry.title: Outlook (new) release notes: June 5, 2026
entry.content: You can now move emails in bulk when exporting to PST files. Copilot chat is now available for third-party accounts with a Copilot license. You can now customize your calendar account names in the left navigation pane. You can now draft and refine emails in Copilot chat using personal Microsoft accounts. Shared calendars assigned by your admin now appear automatically in your calendar list.

entry.title: Furthering Windows as the trusted platform for development
entry.content: New tools and AI-powered capabilities designed to help developers build faster, smarter, and more secure experiences are coming to Windows. These updates focus on making Windows more trustworthy, more productive, and better prepared for the next wave of AI innovation. Microsoft is also expanding the technologies and partnerships that help developers create modern apps that work across devices and cloud-connected experiences. Read the recap of Windows announcements at Microsoft Build to learn how

entry.title: Windows news you can use: May 2026 recap
entry.content: In this month’s edition of Windows news you can use, we’re highlighting updates designed to streamline patching, improve Secure Boot visibility, and simplify cloud management across your Windows environment. Catch up on hotpatch updates now enabled by default for organizations using Windows Autopatch through Microsoft Intune or the Windows updates API, along with expanded hotpatch availability for Windows Server 2025 via Azure Arc at no additional cost. You’ll also find improved Secure Boot visi

entry.title: Outlook (new) release notes: May 29, 2026
entry.content: You can now add attachments and inline images while composing email offline. You can now create inbox rules with more actions, including clearing categories, permanently deleting messages, and managing flags.

entry.title: Outlook Mobile release notes: May 28, 2026
entry.content: Build 5.2620.0 Feature updates iOS You can now use a work week calendar view on iOS and iPad. Select "Work Week" directly from the view picker on iPad or rotate your device to landscape to select on phone. Android Various fixes to functionality and performance.

entry.title: Updated Secure Boot status report in Windows Autopatch
entry.content: Windows Autopatch now delivers deeper, device-level insights into Secure Boot certificate status, trust configuration, and readiness. With new signals like confidence level, alerts, and detailed certificate visibility, you can more confidently move forward with broad updates and targeted remediations. Explore the latest updates to the Secure Boot stats report and learn how to gain more clarity into your Secure Boot rollout.

entry.title: Configuring firewall and proxies for smooth Windows updates
entry.content: If you’re an IT admin, a new guide is here to help you troubleshoot connection issues with the Windows Update service. Some of these issues have to do with the embedded network security design. The key is in the configuration of your network endpoints for firewalls and proxies. Essentially, make sure to trust Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN) subdomains of the recommended DNS subdomains. Dive deeper at Configuring firewall and proxies for smooth Windows updates .

entry.title: Outlook (new) release notes: May 8, 2026
entry.content: You can now book a desk for the entire day in Space Finder. Improved visibility of your organization's logo in dark mode. Copilot chat is now available in pop-out windows, enabling use while reading or composing popped-out messages.

entry.title: Upgrade to Windows Server 2025, now via Windows Update
entry.content: Windows Server supports several methods for moving to a newer version, including in-place upgrade. This method installs a newer version of Windows Server over the existing one, keeping your settings, server roles, features, and data.

entry.title: Dynamically remove apps from managed Windows 11 devices
entry.content: IT admins, you can now simplify Microsoft Store app management with dynamic removal on more devices across your enterprise. Use policy to remove any preinstalled MSIX/APPX app by referencing its Package Family Name (PFN). It’s available to Enterprise and Education devices running Windows 11, versions 25H2 and 24H2. Starting with the April 2026 Windows non-security update, you can use Group Policy or custom OMA-URI for mobile device management (MDM). Additional Microsoft Intune capabilities are c

entry.title: Assess Secure Boot certificate status with Microsoft Defender
entry.content: Microsoft Defender now provides IT teams centralized visibility into Secure Boot 2023 certificate readiness across your device fleet. A new assessment categorizes your devices automatically as exposed, compliant, and not applicable. In the Defender portal, go to Exposure Management > Recommendations > Devices > Misconfigurations . For exposed devices, remediation guidance is directly available through the recommendation. Learn more in Preparing for Secure Boot 2023 with Microsoft Defender .

entry.title: Take action: Out-of-band updates released for Windows Server to address two issues
entry.content: Microsoft has released out-of-band (OOB) updates today, April 19, 2026, to address issues introduced by the April 2026 Windows security update.

entry.title: Interim guidance for Egypt DST changes 2026
entry.content: For Windows devices in Egypt, a workaround is available for an issue affecting Daylight Saving Time (DST). Between April 24 and April 30, 2026, calendar events and meeting times in Egypt will appear off by one hour only for Windows Server 2016. This is due to a calendar edge case: a five-week April in 2026. Clocks will be correctly set to Egypt DST time zone on May 1, 2026, without any further intervention. If needed, a temporary workaround is available for the affected week. Learn more in Inter

Catch up on the general availability of Windows Autopatch update readiness, hotpatch updates now turned on by default for eligible devices, and new RSAT support for Arm‑based Windows 11 PCs. You’ll also find updates on expanded regional availability for Windows 365 Frontline and a p

entry.title: Remote Desktop adds new safeguards for connections opened from RDP files
entry.content: Starting with the April 2026 Windows security update, Remote Desktop adds new safeguards to help reduce phishing attacks that misuse Remote Desktop (.rdp) files. These attacks attempt to trick devices into connecting to attacker‑controlled systems and sharing local resources without clear visibility. To address this, a new security dialog appears before any connection is made, showing the remote computer address, publisher information (when available), and any requested access to local resources

entry.title: Second deployment phase for Kerberos RC4 hardening begins with the April 2026 Windows security update
entry.content: Windows updates released in April 2026 and later begin the second deployment phase of protections designed to address a Kerberos information disclosure vulnerability ( CVE‑2026‑20833 ). This second phase continues the shift away from legacy encryption types such as RC4 by moving toward stronger default ticket behavior. After installing the April 2026 update, domain controllers default to supporting Advanced Encryption Standard (AES‑SHA1) encrypted tickets for accounts that do not have an explici

entry.title: Windows Deployment Services (WDS): Hands-free deployment hardening (Phase 2)
entry.content: As announced in January 2026, the Unattend.xml file used in hands‑free deployment poses a vulnerability when transmitted over an unauthenticated RPC channel. Beginning with the April 2026 security update, the second phase of hardening changes for CVE-2026-0386 is now in effect. These changes make hands‑free deployment disabled by default to enforce secure behavior.

entry.title: Outlook (new) release notes: April 10, 2026
entry.content: Copilot: Get help writing and revising emails with Copilot in chat. Click Help me write or Help me reply in an email draft to get started. Outlook now syncs your email in the background even when the app is closed. To change this, go to Settings > General > Offline and disable the option. You can now undo pin and unpin actions on emails.

entry.title: Windows Office Hours: April 16, 2026
entry.content: If you are an IT admin with questions about managing and updating Windows, we want to help. Every third Thursday of the month, we host a live chat-based event on the Tech Community called Windows Office Hours. Members of the Windows, Microsoft Intune, Windows Autopilot, Windows Autopatch, and Windows 365 engineering teams will be standing by to answer your questions.

entry.title: Hardening administrative actions: Windows imaging, cloning, and auth workflows
entry.content: Administrative actions are undergoing hardening changes that might require operational change to support your organization’s security posture. With the August 2025 Windows non-security update, devices were hardened against unauthorized attempts to bypass loopback detection. However, if you’ve cloned machines without Sysprep, you might see Kerberos and NTLM authentication failures. This is by design. The recommended solution is to rebuild affected devices using supported imaging methods. A tempor

entry.title: Extended Security Updates (ESU) available for Windows 10 Enterprise LTSB 2016
entry.content: If your organization relies on Windows 10 Enterprise LTSB 2016, you know it’s nearing end of support on October 13, 2026. While the long-term solution is upgrading to a newer LTSC release, another temporary solution is also now available. Extended Security Updates (ESU) are now available for purchase through Volume Licensing or a Microsoft Cloud Solution Provider (CSP). Learn more about this transition at Plan for Windows Server 2016 and Windows 10 2016 LTSB end of support .

entry.title: ESR management is moving to Windows Backup for Organizations
entry.content: If your IT department relies on Enterprise State Roaming (ESR) to synchronize user settings, plan for change. Starting May 2026, ESR management is moving to Windows Backup for Organizations. Previously, IT admins were able to access it from the Microsoft Entra portal. This old option will be still available until the end of June 2026. Please start accessing the new default experience through Windows Backup for Organizations policies . Learn more here .

entry.title: Replacement of the Microsoft SaRA command-line utility in Windows
entry.content: If you’re an IT admin, migrate away from the Microsoft Support and Recovery Assistant utility (SaRA) . This command-line utility is deprecated to help secure and harden your environment. The Get Help command-line tool has similar capabilities and is recommended as a replacement for the SaRA utility. You’ll need to download it and use GetHelpCmd.exe to run your scenarios. Learn more at Microsoft Support and Recovery Assistant (SaRA) command-line utility removal from Windows.

entry.title: Outlook (new) release notes: April 3, 2026
entry.content: When editing a table in compose, you can now select an entire row or column using controls at the top and side of the table.

entry.title: Secure Boot certificate update status in the Windows Security app
entry.content: Windows Pro and Home users can now see the status of Secure Boot certificate updates in the Windows Security app. As the 2011 certificates are approaching expiration in 2026, updated 2023 certificates are being delivered automatically through Windows Update. In April 2026, in Device security > Secure Boot , you’ll see a green, yellow, or red badge indicating if you must take action. In May 2026, you’ll also see notifications outside the app, such as system alerts. Learn more about these status a

entry.title: Take Action: Out-of-band update to address an issue affecting installation of the March 2026 non-security preview update
entry.content: Microsoft has identified an issue affecting installation of the March 2026 Windows non-security preview update on some devices running Windows 11, versions 25H2 and 24H2. While installing the update, the following error might occur: “Some update files are missing or have problems. We’ll try to download the update again later. Error code: (0x80073712)”

entry.title: Outlook (new) release notes: March 27, 2026
entry.content: Outlook now requires additional verification when creating email forwarding rules for personal accounts. You can now use categories as conditions when creating inbox rules.

entry.title: New resources to help organizations prepare for Secure Boot certificate expirations
entry.content: Secure Boot certificates begin expiring in June 2026, and IT admins should take action now to help ensure devices remain secure and compliant. Timely deployment of updated certificates is essential to preserve device startup integrity and avoid servicing (i.e., updates) disruptions.

entry.title: Take Action: Out-of-band update released to address an issue signing in to apps with a Microsoft account
entry.content: Microsoft has identified an issue affecting signing in to apps using a Microsoft account . After installing the March 2026 Windows security update, some users might be unable to sign in to Microsoft apps and services. During sign‑in, users might see a “no internet” error even when the device has a working internet connection. This issue affects only Microsoft account sign‑in scenarios; organizations using Microsoft Entra ID for app authentication are not affected.

entry.title: Outlook (new) release notes: March 20, 2026
entry.content: Meeting templates: You can now create personal meeting templates and access templates set by your IT admin from the New Event dropdown in Calendar. You can now import email from PST files in Settings > Files > Import . Newsletters: Outlook now displays a warning when you add external email recipients to a newsletter.

entry.title: Out-of-band hotpatch update released to address an issue affecting Bluetooth device visibility
entry.content: Microsoft has identified an issue affecting hotpatch-enabled Enterprise editions of Windows where Bluetooth devices may not appear on the Bluetooth & devices page in Windows Settings or in Quick Settings , even though the devices are connected and functioning as expected. In addition, users might be unable to add Bluetooth devices because available devices do not appear in the connection list.

entry.title: Out-of-band update released for Windows Enterprise client devices running hotpatch updates
entry.content: Microsoft has identified a security issue in the Windows Routing and Remote Access Service (RRAS) management tool that could allow remote code execution when connecting to a malicious server. This issue only applies to a limited set of scenarios involving Enterprise client devices running hotpatch updates and being used for remote server management.

entry.title: Outlook (new) release notes: March 13, 2026
entry.content: You can now copy a shortened Teams meeting URL from the Copy Link context menu.

entry.title: RSAT capabilities arrive on Arm-based Windows 11 PCs
entry.content: You can now use familiar Remote Server Administration Tools (RSAT) on Arm-based Windows 11 devices. With the February 2026 non-security preview update, five of the most widely used RSAT components are available for everyday system administrator tasks. Add users to Active Directory, edit GPOs, configure NDS and DHCP servers, and more. Learn why RSAT on Arm64 matters, how it’s available on different versions of Windows 11, and how to enable it .

entry.title: Windows Autopatch is enabling hotpatch updates by default
entry.content: Starting with the May 2026 Windows security update, Windows Autopatch is enabling hotpatch security updates by default because they are the quickest way to get secure. This change in default behavior will impact all eligible Microsoft Intune devices. Additional IT controls are coming in April. Read the announcement in Securing devices faster with hotpatch updates on by default .

entry.content: Are you responsible for updating Secure Boot certificates across your organization? They start expiring in June 2026. Tune in next week for best practices and answers to your questions in real time. First up, on March 9 at Microsoft Technical Takeoff is Secure Boot certificate updates explained . This is a fast-paced walkthrough of essential guidance - including how to test firmware, monitor device readiness, deploy updated certificates, and diagnose issues. Have questions? Post them early and o

entry.title: Outlook (new) release notes: March 6, 2026
entry.content: DLP Warn dialog: The Data Loss Prevention (DLP) warn dialog now includes justification, false positive reporting, and acknowledgement fields, aligning with the Outlook for Windows (classic) experience. Time zone display names are now daylight saving time (DST)-aware and show the appropriate standard or daylight time name based on the time of year. You can now assign multiple categories to calendar events and email messages simultaneously using the context menu or ribbon.

entry.title: Windows news you can use: February 2026 recap
entry.content: In this month’s edition of Windows news you can use, we’re helping you catch up on upcoming events, new resources to help with Secure Boot certificate updates, and more. Explore the latest capabilities in Windows Backup for Organizations. Learn how Windows 365 Boot provide a differentiated experience for Windows 365 Reserve. If you’re an IT admin looking for what’s new in Windows device management, security, and cloud experiences for the enterprise, this is a good place to start.

entry.title: Prepare devices for successful updates with Windows Autopatch update readiness
entry.content: Windows Autopatch update readiness helps IT admins proactively identify and resolve issues that can block Windows updates before deployment. It provides visibility into device readiness across the Microsoft Intune-managed Windows fleet and surfaces actionable insights to reduce update failures and manual troubleshooting. By shifting update management from reactive to proactive, it helps improve update success rates and strengthen overall fleet reliability.

entry.title: Outlook (new) release notes: February 27, 2026
entry.content: A new content picker pane lets you insert images, stickers, and emojis when composing a message. When you share a folder, Outlook now checks if the recipient needs access to the parent folder and prompts you to share it when necessary. The emoji picker (shown when you type ":") has an updated design. An informational banner now appears when you navigate from an encrypted email to a non-encrypted email. A hover button is now available on group headers in your message list for quick access to grou

entry.content: New documentation is now available to help IT administrators prepare for Secure Boot certificate updates and manage update readiness across virtual and cloud‑managed Windows environments. This documentation includes Secure Boot certificate updates for Azure Virtual Desktop , Windows 365 Cloud PCs , and monitoring Secure Boot certificate status using Microsoft Intune remediations , providing visibility into readiness across managed devices. entry.content: Learn about tools and options available to organizations to update Secure Boot certificates on Windows Server. Certificates begin expiring in June 2026. You must update them before that date to help keep your security posture. Many recent platforms already include the supported 2023 certificates in firmware. entry.title: Outlook (new) release notes: February 20, 2026
entry.content: Search: Improvements to search scope to allow folder or subfolder selection. Edit appointment titles inline on the calendar surface (in week view grid and for all-day events).

entry.title: Personalize your Message center feed
entry.content: The Message center in the Microsoft 365 admin center includes personalization features that help IT admins focus on Windows updates and reduce information overload. By adjusting service preferences, filters, and columns, IT admins can prioritize Windows feature updates, security and out‑of‑band updates, hardening changes, and lifecycle announcements most relevant to their environments.

entry.title: Coming soon to organizations: Customize the Start menu with updated policies
entry.content: The new Start menu is gradually rolling out to general audiences and will be available to organizations in the second quarter of the calendar year. Two policies allow IT admins to further customize the Start menu: HideCategoryView and ConfigureStartPins. Learn more about them at Start Policy CSP and Start policy settings .

Declined events: If “Show declined events in your calendar” is enabled in New Outlook on your desktop, you can hide declined events in the app to declutter your mobile calendar. Use the Calendar’s Filter to show or hide declined events in the mobile app without affecting their display on your desktop. You can now bo

entry.title: Outlook (new) release notes: February 13, 2026

Every third Thursday of the month, we host a live chat-based event on the Tech Community called Windows Office Hours. Members of the Windows, Microsoft Intune, Windows Autopilot, Windows Autopatch, and Windows 365 engineering teams will be standing by to answer your questions. We also have experts from FastTrack, the Customer Acceleration Team, and Microsoft public sector teams.

entry.title: Refreshing the root of trust: industry collaboration on Secure Boot certificate updates
entry.content: Secure Boot is a foundational Windows security feature that runs at startup, before Windows load, and helps ensure that only trusted, digitally signed software can execute. After more than 15 years of continuous service, the original Secure Boot certificates are reaching the end of their planned lifecycle and begin expiring in late June 2026.

entry.content: 11:30 am PT update: Microsoft Store and Windows Update services have been restored. Some IT administrators may continue to experience residual latency affecting Windows Server update operations, including the Windows Server sync API. This Server‑specific issue is being addressed separately. If you experience this latency, please contact Microsoft Support for business .

entry.title: Outlook (new) release notes: February 6, 2026
entry.content: Various fixes to functionality and performance.

entry.title: Advancing Windows security: Disabling NTLM by default
entry.content: Windows is moving toward a more secure authentication model by phasing out New Technology LAN Manager (NTLM) in favor of stronger, Kerberos‑based alternatives. This transition is taking a three-phased approach, leading toward disabling NTLM by default in upcoming Windows releases. With each phase come new capabilities so that your organization has the tools, visibility, and compatibility support needed. Learn more at Advancing Windows security: Disabling NTLM by default.

entry.title: Windows news you can use: January 2026 recap
entry.content: In this month’s edition of Windows news you can use, we’re helping you catch up on important changes and evolving standards that will help strengthen the security of the Windows ecosystem across client, cloud, and server. Learn about new tools to help with Secure Boot certificate updates and a new command-line tool for Windows app developers. Explore features and enhancements that will start gradually rolling out in February, which include improvements to Cross-Device Resume, Narrator, Voice Acc

entry.title: Skill up on Windows at Technical Takeoff
entry.content: If you’re looking for prescriptive guidance help you confidently configure, manage, and support Windows in your organization, mark your calendars for Microsoft Technical Takeoff 2026. Taking place on the first four Mondays in March, this free event offers technical deep dives, AMAs, and feedback sessions—all supported by live Q&A with the teams behind the features. See what’s in store for Windows or browse the full agenda .

entry.title: Secure Boot status report in Windows Autopatch
entry.content: If you’re an IT admin using Windows Autopatch, a new report is now available to monitor the status of Secure Boot across your organization. As the 2011 Secure Boot certificates start expiring in June 2026 , this report helps you start by identifying devices that have Secure Boot enabled. Out of those, see which devices are fully up to date and which ones still need updated certificates. Learn more and get started with this report at Secure Boot status report in Windows Autopatch .

entry.title: Take Action: Out-of-band update to address cloud‑backed storage application issues
entry.content: Microsoft released today a resolution for an issue observed after installing the January 2026 Windows security update. This issue may cause applications that open or save files stored in cloud‑backed locations to become unresponsive or display errors. Some installations of Outlook may also become unresponsive and fail to open when PST files are stored in cloud‑backed storage such as OneDrive.

entry.title: Windows Backup for Organizations expands to first sign-in restore
entry.content: Preview the expanded Windows Backup for Organizations experience! In early 2026, if users miss the restore option during the out-of-box experience (OOBE) by accident or due to a technical issue, they can still restore at first sign-in. Windows Backup for Organizations helps preserve Windows settings, the list of installed Microsoft Store apps, and the Start menu pins. And now, even more devices can take advantage of this capability, including Microsoft Entra hybrid joined, multi-user setups, and

entry.title: The January 2026 Windows security update is now available
entry.content: The January 2026 security update is now available for all supported versions of Windows. We recommend that you install these updates promptly. Information about the contents of this update is available from the release notes, which are accessible from the Windows 11 update history page. For instructions on how to install this update on your home device, check the Update Windows article. entry.title: Hardening changes coming to Common Log File System (CLFS) authentication
entry.content: Windows 11, version 25H2 and Windows Server 2025 updates released on or after October 28, 2025 include a new hardening authentication mitigation for the Common Log File System (CLFS) driver. This change adds a hash-based message authentication code (HMAC) to the underlying files of a CLFS logfile. Authentication codes are generated by combining file data with a system-unique cryptographic key stored in the registry, accessible only to administrators and SYSTEM accounts. This mechanism allows CLF

entry.content: Note Version numbers will no longer be included in release notes. Features in these notes roll out to all versions automatically. Increase in options to delay send (from 10 seconds to 30 seconds) to give you more time to undo sending an email. (Edit Feb 20: Corrected number of seconds) Personal dictionary under Editor settings to improve spellcheck and text suggestions. entry.title: Windows Office Hours: January 15, 2026
entry.content: If you are an IT admin with questions about managing and updating Windows, we want to help. Every third Thursday of the month, we host a live chat-based event on the Tech Community called Windows Office Hours. Members of the Windows, Microsoft Intune, Windows Autopilot, Windows Autopatch, and Windows 365 engineering teams will be standing by to answer your questions. We also have experts from FastTrack, the Customer Acceleration Team, and Microsoft public sector teams.

entry.title: Get started with December 2025 improvements in Windows 11
entry.content: Start using the newest Windows 11 capabilities with helpful tips and actionable steps summarized in one place. Discover IT resources to prepare for Secure Boot certificate update, track and remediate CVEs in Windows Autopatch, opt in to native NVMe support in Windows Server 2025, and much more to help keep your organization protected and productive.

entry.title: Take Action: Out-of-band update to address MSMQ service issues leading to queue operations and resource-related errors
entry.content: Microsoft has identified an issue affecting Message Queuing (MSMQ) functionality in some enterprise environments after installing the December 2025 Windows security update (released December 9, 2025). Individuals using Windows Home or Pro editions on personal devices are very unlikely to experience this issue, as it primarily affects enterprise or managed IT environments.

entry.title: Latest on Windows quality updates out of the box – now disabled by default
entry.content: Starting with the January 2026 security update, the AllowOOBEUpdates CSP policy will be available to IT admins and disabled by default. It shows up as a new setting on the Windows Autopilot Enrollment Status Page (ESP). This policy allows you to install the latest Windows quality updates during the out-of-box experience (OOBE) on eligible devices. Devices must be Microsoft Entra joined or Microsoft Entra hybrid joined and running Windows 11, version 22H2 or later. Read the updated announcement i

entry.title: How to use Microsoft Intune to update expiring Secure Boot certificates
entry.content: If you’re an IT admin, you can now deploy, manage, and monitor Secure Boot certificate updates using Microsoft Intune. This method represents an alternative to setting registry keys and using Group Policy. You can now use Intune to trigger deployment on all domain-joined Windows clients, opt out of high-confidence buckets, and opt in to Microsoft managing these updates.

entry.title: Outlook (new) release notes: December 5, 2025

entry.title: Secure Boot AMA: Ask Microsoft Anything on December 10
entry.content: Join us December 10, 2025, at 8:00 AM PST for a live Ask Microsoft Anything (AMA) session focused on updating Secure Boot certificates on your Windows devices before they expire in June 2026. This event gives IT admins the chance to ask questions and get expert guidance on Secure Boot configuration, update scenarios, inventorying and preparing your environment, and formulating the right deployment plan for your organization. Members of the Windows team will be available to answer your questions,

entry.title: New Windows Autopatch reports on CVEs
entry.content: A new Windows Autopatch report empowers your organization to prioritize update deployment, demonstrate compliance, and maintain a robust security posture. The Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) report joins your other Windows quality update reports right in the Microsoft Intune admin center. Use it to view Windows CVEs addressed by recent quality updates and follow links to remediation documentation and device-level vulnerability status. Get started at New Windows Autopatch reports on C

entry.title: Outlook (new) release notes: May 30, 2025
entry.content: Version 20250523006.11 Offline Days of email to save expanded to 30 days: The default mail sync window in offline mode has been expanded from seven days to 30 days. Offline search folders: Search folders, which help users group emails based on certain criteria, are now available while offline. Move emails between accounts: Consumer users can now seamlessly move emails between their personal accounts, streamlining organization and productivity.
